title: Video-CoE: Reinforcing Video Event Prediction via Chain of Events
task: Video event prediction via chain-of-events reasoning
key_results: Outperforms leading MLLMs on VEP benchmarks; state-of-the-art on public datasets
comments: The CoE paradigm explicitly constructs temporal event chains to force visual focus and logical reasoning, addressing identified MLLM weaknesses in future-event prediction.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Constructing explicit temporal event chains improves MLLM reasoning for future-event prediction
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; CoE outperforms baselines across public benchmarks

title: MetaClaw: Just Talk -- An Agent That Meta-Learns and Evolves in the Wild
task: Continual meta-learning for evolving LLM agents
key_results: 32% relative accuracy improvement via skill-driven adaptation; full pipeline improves Kimi-K2.5 from 21.4% to 40.6%
comments: MetaClaw uniquely addresses online agent evolution without downtime through decoupled skill synthesis and opportunistic policy optimization.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Skill-driven fast adaptation enables immediate improvement without policy retraining
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 32% relative improvement with zero downtime

hypotheses.hypothesis: Opportunistic policy optimization during user-inactive windows improves overall performance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; full pipeline advances from 21.4% to 40.6% accuracy

title: MosaicMem: Hybrid Spatial Memory for Controllable Video World Models
task: Hybrid spatial memory for consistent video world models
key_results: Improved pose adherence vs implicit memory; stronger dynamic modeling than explicit baselines; enables minute-level navigation and scene editing
comments: MosaicMem cleverly combines 3D patch lifting for reliable localization with implicit model conditioning for flexible generation, balancing consistency and dynamics.

model_io.model: Video diffusion model + MosaicMem hybrid memory

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hybrid spatial memory combining 3D structure with implicit conditioning improves both consistency and dynamic modeling
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; outperforms explicit and implicit baselines

title: Alignment Makes Language Models Normative, Not Descriptive
task: Analyzing alignment's effect on LLM descriptiveness vs normativity
key_results: Base models predict human choices ~10:1 better in multi-round games; aligned models dominate in one-shot normative settings
comments: This reveals a fundamental trade-off: alignment optimizes for human preferences but sacrifices behavioral prediction fidelity in strategic, history-dependent contexts.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Alignment shifts LLMs toward normative rather than descriptive modeling
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; base models predict multi-round behavior better, aligned models predict one-shot normative behavior better

hypotheses.hypothesis: The normative bias depends on whether human behavior is structured by game theory or history
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; pattern reverses at round one within multi-round games

title: Complementary Reinforcement Learning
task: Experience-driven agent learning via complementary RL
key_results: 10% performance improvement in single-task scenarios; robust scalability in multi-task settings
comments: Complementary RL elegantly co-evolves experience extraction and policy in lockstep, inspired by neuroscience principles, addressing a key limitation of static experience stores.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Co-evolving experience extraction with policy prevents actor-experience misalignment
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 10% improvement over outcome-only baselines

title: GigaWorld-Policy: An Efficient Action-Centered World--Action Model
task: Action-centered world-action model for robot policy learning
key_results: 9x faster than Motus baseline; 7% improvement in task success; 95% improvement vs pi-0.5 on RoboTwin 2.0
comments: GigaWorld-Policy ingeniously decouples action and video generation with causal masking, enabling efficient inference while maintaining visual-dynamics supervision.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Causal masking preventing future-video influence on actions enables optional video generation without harming action inference
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 9x speedup with 7% success improvement

title: Look Before Acting: Enhancing Vision Foundation Representations for Vision-Language-Action Models
task: Enhanced visual understanding for vision-language-action models
key_results: 9.0% improvement on simulated tasks; 7.5% on real-world tasks vs prior SoTA
comments: DeepVision-VLA identifies and addresses the critical insight that visual token sensitivity decreases in deeper MLLM layers, enabling targeted visual enhancement.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Injecting multi-level visual features into deeper VLA layers enhances visual grounding for manipulation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 9.0% simulated, 7.5% real-world gains

hypotheses.hypothesis: Pruning irrelevant tokens via shallow-layer attention preserves task-critical visual cues
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; AGVP reduces tokens with minimal overhead

title: When AI Navigates the Fog of War
task: LLM reasoning about unfolding geopolitical crises under fog of war
key_results: Models display strategic realism in economically structured settings; uneven across political domains; narratives evolve from containment to entrenchment
comments: Temporally grounded case study of 2026 Middle East conflict mitigates training-data leakage, providing archival insight into how frontier LLMs reason about ongoing crises.

hypotheses.hypothesis: LLMs reason with strategic realism toward deeper structural incentives in geopolitical conflicts
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; models display sophisticated reasoning beyond surface rhetoric

hypotheses.hypothesis: Model prediction accuracy is higher in economically structured vs politically ambiguous domains
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; uneven reliability across domains

title: Temporal Gains, Spatial Costs: Revisiting Video Fine-Tuning in Multimodal Large Language Models
task: Understanding spatial-temporal trade-offs in video fine-tuning of MLLMs
key_results: Video-SFT improves video performance but yields limited gains or degradation on static images; temporal budget directly linked to trade-off
comments: This systematic study reveals Video-SFT is not a free lunch, challenging the assumption that joint image-video training universally benefits both modalities.

model_io.model: MLLM with video and image components

hypotheses.hypothesis: Video-SFT trades spatial understanding for temporal understanding
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; consistent pattern across architectures and scales

hypotheses.hypothesis: Increasing frame count improves video performance but not image performance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; temporal budget drives the trade-off

title: BenchPreS: A Benchmark for Context-Aware Personalized Preference Selectivity of Persistent-Memory LLMs
task: Context-aware preference application in persistent-memory LLMs
key_results: Frontier LLMs struggle with context-sensitive preference application; models with stronger preference adherence show higher misapplication rates
comments: BenchPreS reveals that memory-augmented LLMs treat personalized preferences as globally enforceable rules, not context-dependent signals—a critical design flaw for production systems.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Frontier LLMs struggle to suppress preferences in third-party communication contexts
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; high misapplication rates even with reasoning

title: LoST: Level of Semantics Tokenization for 3D Shapes
task: Semantic-level tokenization for 3D shape generation
key_results: Surpasses LoD-based tokenizers on geometric/semantic metrics; achieves efficient AR generation with 0.1%-10% tokens of prior models
comments: LoST innovatively reorders tokens by semantic salience, enabling early prefixes to decode into plausible complete shapes, a paradigm shift from spatial LoD hierarchies.

model_io.model: Latent VAE + semantic tokenizer + AR decoder

hypotheses.hypothesis: Semantic-level tokenization outperforms geometric level-of-detail hierarchies for AR modeling
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SoTA reconstruction metrics, efficient generation

title: ESPIRE: A Diagnostic Benchmark for Embodied Spatial Reasoning of Vision-Language Models
task: Diagnostic benchmark for embodied spatial reasoning in VLMs
key_results: Comprehensive evaluation across spatial-reasoning-centric robotic tasks; fine-grained analysis of localization and execution capabilities
comments: ESPIRE's decomposition of tasks into localization and execution with generative evaluation (vs discriminative QA) provides deeper insights into actionable spatial reasoning.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Generative evaluation of spatial reasoning better captures actionable understanding than discriminative QA
hypotheses.result: Implied by benchmark design; stronger diagnosis of reasoning-to-act capabilities

title: Conservative Offline Robot Policy Learning via Posterior-Transition Reweighting
task: Conservative offline robot policy learning with heterogeneous data
key_results: PTR reallocates credit to high-quality trajectories; compatible with diffusion and flow-matching action heads
comments: PTR elegantly solves the heterogeneous robot data problem by using transition scoring to estimate sample attributability, avoiding uniform credit assignment.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Posterior-transition reweighting improves adaptation to heterogeneous robot data vs uniform weighting
hypotheses.result: Implied; method designed to handle conflicting/low-quality demonstrations

title: Stereo World Model: Camera-Guided Stereo Video Generation
task: Camera-conditioned stereo video generation with consistent geometry
key_results: 3x faster generation; 5% gain in viewpoint consistency; improved disparity accuracy vs monocular-then-convert pipelines
comments: StereoWorld's stereo-aware attention decomposition leveraging epipolar priors elegantly achieves efficient consistent stereo generation while maintaining pretrained video priors.

model_io.model: Video diffusion + stereo-aware attention + camera RoPE

hypotheses.hypothesis: Stereo-aware attention factorization with epipolar priors improves consistency and efficiency
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 3x speedup, 5% viewpoint consistency gain

title: AdaMem: Adaptive User-Centric Memory for Long-Horizon Dialogue Agents
task: Adaptive user-centric memory for long-horizon dialogue agents
key_results: SoTA on LoCoMo and PERSONAMEM benchmarks; organized memory across working, episodic, persona, and graph modalities
comments: AdaMem's question-conditioned retrieval combining semantic search with selective graph expansion elegantly balances recall and relevance for user modeling.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-modal memory (working, episodic, persona, graph) outperforms single-mode approaches
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SoTA on both benchmarks

title: Unified Spatio-Temporal Token Scoring for Efficient Video VLMs
task: Unified spatio-temporal token pruning for efficient video VLMs
key_results: Prunes 50% of vision tokens; 62% efficiency improvement with 0.7% performance drop; scales with frame count
comments: STTS's lightweight architecture-wide pruning without text conditioning or token merging is elegantly simple while achieving substantial efficiency gains across 13 video QA tasks.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Unified spatio-temporal token scoring across ViT and LLM improves efficiency without text conditioning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 62% efficiency gain with minimal performance loss

title: LaDe: Unified Multi-Layered Graphic Media Generation and Decomposition
task: Unified multi-layered graphic media generation and decomposition
key_results: Outperforms Qwen-Image-Layered on text-to-layers and image-to-layers tasks; supports flexible layer counts
comments: LaDe's unified framework handling generation, decomposition, and text-to-layers via 4D RoPE conditioning elegantly sidesteps fixed layer-count limitations.

model_io.model: Latent diffusion transformer + RGBA VAE + LLM expander

hypotheses.hypothesis: Unified generation and decomposition with flexible layers outperforms fixed-layer approaches
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; improvements on text-to-layers and image-to-layers

title: Efficient Exploration at Scale
task: Efficient data-driven RLHF via information-directed exploration
key_results: Matches offline RLHF on 200K labels using <20K labels (10x gain); extrapolates to 1000x gain at scale
comments: The combination of epistemic neural networks, nudged reinforcement signals, and information-directed exploration dramatically improves RLHF sample efficiency.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Information-directed exploration with epistemic uncertainty reduces sample complexity in RLHF
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 10x+ improvement in data efficiency

title: VideoAtlas: Navigating Long-Form Video in Logarithmic Compute
task: Navigating long-form video via hierarchical grid with logarithmic compute
key_results: Logarithmic compute growth with video duration; 30-60% multimodal cache hit rate; duration-robust to 10-hour benchmarks
comments: VideoAtlas's hierarchical grid representation enabling recursive navigation is elegant and addresses the critical challenge of long-form video understanding without lossy compression.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hierarchical grid navigation ensures logarithmic compute growth with video length
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; demonstrated on 1-10 hour video ranges

title: Efficient Training-Free Multi-Token Prediction via Embedding-Space Probing
task: Multi-token prediction via embedding-space probing without training
key_results: 12% acceptance-length improvement on LLaMA3; 8-12% on Qwen3; 15-19% throughput gains
comments: The key insight that mask-token representations naturally align with next-token states enables training-free parallel prediction, avoiding costly draft models.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Decoder layers naturally align mask-token representations with next-token states
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; enables accurate MTP without retraining

title: RAMP: Reinforcement Adaptive Mixed Precision Quantization for Efficient On Device LLM Inference
task: Reinforcement learning for adaptive mixed-precision LLM quantization
key_results: 5.54 perplexity at 3.68GB (3.65 bits) on Llama2-7B; zero-shot transfer to Llama2-13B and Mistral; 99.5% FP16 performance on edge
comments: RAMP's off-policy SAC framework learns per-layer bit assignments conditioned on activation statistics, enabling practical sub-4bit quantization with zero-shot generalization.

hypotheses.hypothesis: RL-learned mixed-precision outperforms uniform quantization under bit budgets
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; outperforms AWQ and GPTQ

hypotheses.hypothesis: Quantization sensitivity is primarily architectural, enabling zero-shot transfer
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Llama2-7B policy transfers to 13B and Mistral

title: FINER: MLLMs Hallucinate under Fine-grained Negative Queries
task: Identifying and mitigating hallucinations in MLLMs under fine-grained queries
key_results: Up to 24.2% gains (InternVL3.5) on FINER benchmarks; improvements on 8 existing hallucination suites; 6 general benchmarks
comments: FINER-Tuning with DPO on fine-grained negative queries elegantly targets the core hallucination mechanism—mismatches co-occurring with present elements.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fine-grained negative queries effectively identify and mitigate hallucinations via DPO
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; up to 24.2% improvement with broad generalization

title: ACE-LoRA: Graph-Attentive Context Enhancement for Parameter-Efficient Adaptation of Medical Vision-Language Models
task: Parameter-efficient adaptation of generalist medical vision-language models
key_results: Outperforms SoTA on zero-shot classification, segmentation, detection across multiple domains with only 0.95M parameters
comments: ACE-LoRA elegantly bridges specialization-generalization by using hypergraph attention to capture higher-order diagnostic interactions, avoiding generic pairwise similarity.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Higher-order contextual interactions via hypergraph attention enhance diagnostic fine-grained cues
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SoTA across three tasks with minimal parameters

title: AI Scientist via Synthetic Task Scaling
task: Synthetic task generation and scaling for training ML research agents
key_results: 9% improvement (Qwen3-4B) and 12% (Qwen3-8B) on MLGym via student training on synthetic trajectories
comments: Automatic synthesis of grounded, verified ML tasks enables practical agent training at scale, though synthetic task quality remains a limiting factor.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Synthetic ML tasks grounded in real datasets enable effective agent training
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 9-12% improvements on MLGym

title: HeBA: Heterogeneous Bottleneck Adapters for Robust Vision-Language Models
task: Heterogeneous bottleneck adapters for robust vision-language model adaptation
key_results: SoTA on 11 few-shot benchmarks; modality-specific architectures outperform homogeneous approaches
comments: HeBA's key insight that vision and text require distinct structural inductive biases (spatial convolutions vs semantic projections) is well-motivated and empirically validated.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Modality-specific heterogeneous adapters outperform homogeneous designs
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SoTA on 11 few-shot benchmarks

hypotheses.hypothesis: Bottleneck regularization improves robustness vs expanding adapters
hypotheses.result: Implied; achieves superior stability

title: PRISM: Demystifying Retention and Interaction in Mid-Training
task: Comprehensive analysis of mid-training design for LLM reasoning enhancement
key_results: +15 to +40 points on math, +5 to +12 on code, +6 to +13 on science; PRISM-RL pipeline achieves 3-4x improvement on reasoning benchmarks
comments: PRISM's controlled experiments across seven base models reveal that data composition and mid-training's dense representational restructuring matter far more than RL configuration.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Mid-training composition (science data) matters more than RL mix for reasoning gains
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; +17 to +28 GPQA gains from mid-training data vs <2 from RL

hypotheses.hypothesis: Mid-training restructures model weights densely while RL refines sparsely
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; CKA analysis shows 90%+ weight changes vs 5% for RL

title: Coherent Human-Scene Reconstruction from Multi-Person Multi-View Video in a Single Pass
task: Multi-person multi-view 3D human and scene reconstruction from video
key_results: Competitive performance on EMDB, RICH, EgoHumans, and EgoExo4D; 8x faster than optimization-based multi-view approaches
comments: Unified single-pass framework avoiding external modules is elegant, though scale adjustment module suggests fundamental challenges in multi-scale reasoning remain.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Geometry-based multi-person association is more robust than appearance-based methods
hypotheses.result: Confirmed through evaluation on multi-person datasets

hypotheses.hypothesis: Scale adjustment module can resolve discrepancies between human and scene scales
hypotheses.result: Demonstrated effectiveness on benchmarks with varying human-scene scale ratios

title: Fanar-Sadiq: A Multi-Agent Architecture for Grounded Islamic QA
task: Multi-agent architecture for grounded Islamic question-answering
key_results: Bilingual (Arabic/English) system with intent-aware routing, retrieval-grounded fiqh answers, verse lookup, and deterministic calculators; ~1.9M API accesses in <1 year
comments: Fanar-Sadiq elegantly routes queries to specialized modules (RAG, calculators, deterministic rule engines) rather than relying on a single LLM, ensuring grounding and correctness.

model_io.model: Multi-agent Islamic assistant with specialized modules

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-agent routing to specialized modules improves grounding and reduces hallucination
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; system is production-deployed with high engagement

title: Engineering synthetic cells with intramembrane domains possessing distinct bilayer asymmetries
task: Engineer synthetic cells with spatially-confined membrane asymmetries using inverted emulsion method
key_results: Inverted emulsion produces synthetic cells with symmetric, asymmetric, and phase-separated bilayers with distinct lateral asymmetries; asymmetric domains show pronounced curvature, budding, and division; asymmetry validated via protein-lipid interaction and quenching assays
comments: This biomimetic approach successfully recapitulates key membrane organization principles, though the spontaneous budding/division phenotype requires mechanistic clarification regarding membrane tension and curvature coupling.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Inverted emulsion method can reliably produce membranes with laterally-confined asymmetric domains
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; phase-separated domains with distinct bilayer asymmetries generated

hypotheses.hypothesis: Membrane asymmetry promotes curvature and membrane division in synthetic cells
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; asymmetric and phase-separated membranes display pronounced curvature, budding, and division

title: Reversible DNA condensation drives natural transformation
task: Elucidate ComEA-mediated DNA condensation and decondensation mechanism during bacterial natural transformation
key_results: ComEA forms two conformational states: bridging oligomers (sparse decoration) generating sub-piconewton DNA condensation forces; non-bridging oligomers (dense decoration) decondensing DNA without force; mutations favoring either state cause transformation deficiency
comments: Single-molecule optical tweezers elegantly reveal the mechanical switching mechanism underlying DNA transport; the reversible condensation-decondensation cycle is a sophisticated regulatory mechanism for controlling DNA transit.

hypotheses.hypothesis: ComEA undergoes conformation-dependent oligomerization to reversibly condense DNA
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; bridging oligomers condense, non-bridging oligomers decondense

hypotheses.hypothesis: Both condensation and decondensation phases are mechanistically essential for transformation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; mutations locking either conformation impair transformation efficiency

title: Membrane interfacial potential governs surface condensation and fibrillation of α-Synuclein in neurons
task: Determine how lipid membrane interfaces promote α-Synuclein phase separation and aggregation
key_results: α-Synuclein condenses at sub-critical concentrations (~10 nM) on specific lipid stoichiometries; condensates nucleate fibrils and trigger membrane deformation; membrane depolarization alters puncta size/number and releases protein from neurons
comments: The prewetting-like transition model elegantly explains how interfacial potential drives condensation below bulk saturation, with direct relevance to PD pathogenesis mechanisms.

model_io.model: In vitro lipid bilayer + α-Synuclein reconstitution

hypotheses.hypothesis: Lipid membrane interfacial potential drives local phase separation of α-Synuclein below bulk saturation concentration
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; prewetting-like transition observed with specific lipid stoichiometries

hypotheses.hypothesis: Condensates serve as nucleation sites for fibril formation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; fibrils form from membrane-localized condensates causing membrane damage

title: Exploring proteins and protein–ligand complexes through residue interaction networks
task: Develop and describe residue interaction network (RIN) analysis for protein structure interpretation
key_results: RING software condenses structural data into noncovalent interaction subsets enabling rapid biological insight; complete workflow executable in <45 min for single and multi-state protein analyses
comments: This is a protocol paper providing practical guidance rather than novel mechanistic insights; useful for streamlining structure analysis but limited in experimental innovation.

title: Riboswitch-controlled lipid conversion enables functional membrane asymmetry in artificial cells
task: Engineer stimulus-responsive lipid remodeling in artificial cells via riboswitch-controlled enzyme expression
key_results: Fluoride-responsive riboswitch triggers PLD expression converting PC to PA; creates asymmetric negatively-charged membrane enabling MscL channel incorporation; kinetics characterized across varying DNA and fluoride concentrations
comments: This work demonstrates programmable membrane remodeling in cell-free systems, though scalability and robustness of riboswitch switching kinetics warrant further optimization.

model_io.model: Cell-free expression system in lipid vesicles

hypotheses.hypothesis: Riboswitch-mediated PLD expression can dynamically remodel membrane lipid composition
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; PC→PA conversion kinetics achieved with fluoride induction

hypotheses.hypothesis: Lipid asymmetry enables functional incorporation of mechanosensitive channels
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; asymmetric membranes support MscL channel activity

title: PRSS22 inhibits HMOX1-mediated ferroptosis and induces osteopontin cleavage to promote M2 macrophage polarization and colitis-associated carcinogenesis
task: Identify PRSS22 as driver of inflammation-cancer transition in CRC by suppressing HMOX1-mediated ferroptosis and promoting macrophage M2 polarization
key_results: PRSS22 knockdown increased HMOX1 expression inducing ferroptosis and redox stress in CRC cells; PRSS22 cleaves osteopontin reducing migration; loss of PRSS22 suppressed M0-to-M2 macrophage polarization in co-culture assays
comments: The multi-level mechanistic integration is comprehensive, but the direct substrates of PRSS22 beyond osteopontin and the structural basis of ferroptosis inhibition require additional proteomic and biochemical characterization.

model_io.model: Co-culture of CRC cells and THP-1-derived macrophages with PRSS22 modulation

hypotheses.hypothesis: PRSS22 suppresses HMOX1 expression enabling tumor progression via ferroptosis inhibition
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: PRSS22 knockdown promoted HMOX1 expression and ferroptosis

hypotheses.hypothesis: PRSS22-mediated osteopontin cleavage promotes macrophage M2 polarization and CRC migration
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: Loss of PRSS22 prevented osteopontin cleavage and reduced M2 polarization and migration

title: A feedback mechanism from prostate cancer cells to macrophages, reinforced by STAT1, regulates tumor progression and resistance to radiotherapy
task: Decipher STAT1-mediated lactate-driven crosstalk between prostate cancer and macrophages in radiotherapy resistance
key_results: STAT1 increased glycolytic enzyme expression driving lactate secretion; lactate activated macrophage NFκB1 promoting M2 polarization and MCP-1 secretion; MCP-1/CCR2 signaling activated JAK/STAT1 in cancer cells
comments: The positive feedback loop is elegantly mapped but requires validation that disrupting any single node (STAT1, MCT1, NFκB1, or CCR2) is sufficient to restore radiotherapy sensitivity in vivo.

model_io.model: Prostate cancer cells and bone marrow-derived macrophages co-culture

hypotheses.hypothesis: STAT1-driven lactate secretion activates macrophage NFκB1 pathway promoting M2 polarization
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: Lactate transport via MCT1 activated NFκB1 and induced M2 markers

hypotheses.hypothesis: Macrophage-derived MCP-1 activates CCR2/JAK/STAT1 axis in cancer cells completing positive feedback
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: MCP-1 treatment activated JAK/STAT1 in cancer cells

title: K48 and K63 linkage-competed ubiquitination of BECN1 promotes circPDE4D-mediated autophagy in chronic obstructive pulmonary disease
task: Elucidate circPDE4D-mediated autophagy regulation through K48/K63 ubiquitination competition on BECN1 in COPD
key_results: circPDE4D downregulated in COPD correlates with reduced lung function; circPDE4D/miR545-3p/SMURF1 axis competes K63 vs K48 ubiquitination of BECN1; K63-linked ubiquitination enhances BECN1 stability and autophagy promoting inflammation relief
comments: The mechanistic loop is comprehensive but the in vivo therapeutic potential requires demonstration that circPDE4D restoration or miR545-3p inhibition improves COPD outcomes in animal models.

model_io.model: COPD patient samples and cell culture models

hypotheses.hypothesis: circPDE4D suppresses miR545-3p regulating SMURF1 and BECN1 ubiquitination
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: circPDE4D acts as miR545-3p sponge promoting SMURF1 expression and K63-linked BECN1 ubiquitination

hypotheses.hypothesis: K63-linked BECN1 ubiquitination enhances stability and autophagy relieving COPD inflammation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: circPDE4D promoted autophagy and reduced inflammation in vitro and in vivo

title: Acetylation-triggered degradation of MSX1 impairs palatal development
task: Elucidate SIRT1-mediated MSX1 acetylation as a proteostatic switch governing embryonic palatal development and cleft palate pathogenesis
key_results: MSX1 hyperacetylation in atRA-induced cleft palate drives proteasomal degradation triggering EPM apoptosis; SIRT1 suppression promotes MSX1 acetylation; SIRT1 overexpression or MSX1 K139R deacetylation mimic reduced cleft severity
comments: The acetylation-dependent proteostatic regulation is novel and mechanistically distinct from transcriptional PTM effects; however, the precise acetyl-transferase and the structural basis of acetylation-triggered degradation require identification.

model_io.model: atRA-induced embryonic palatal mesenchymal (EPM) cells and tissues
Mouse palatal development with lentivirus-mediated SIRT1 or MSX1 K139R delivery

hypotheses.hypothesis: SIRT1-catalyzed MSX1 deacetylation preserves MSX1 protein stability and EPM survival during palatal development
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: SIRT1 suppression drove MSX1 hyperacetylation and degradation; SIRT1 restoration reduced cleft severity

hypotheses.hypothesis: Acetylation-mediated MSX1 degradation is the primary pathogenic mechanism in atRA-induced cleft palate
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: Transcriptomic profiling revealed exclusively proteostatic role of acetylation distinct from transcriptional effects

title: IVNS1ABP mutation drives cellular senescence in newly identified progeroid neuropathy
task: Determine cellular mechanisms of IVNS1ABP mutation-induced premature senescence in progeroid neuropathy
key_results: Mutant IVNS1ABP caused defective cytokinesis, increased DNA damage, and premature senescence in patient-derived fibroblasts, iPSCs, and NPCs; mutation altered binding to actin/actin-associated proteins dysregulating actin dynamics
comments: The cytokinesis defect is mechanistically linked to actin dysregulation, but the direct substrate interactions and structural basis of mutant protein dysfunction require further biochemical characterization.

model_io.model: Patient-derived iPSCs and isogenic controls differentiated to NPCs

hypotheses.hypothesis: Mutant IVNS1ABP dysregulates actin dynamics causing cytokinesis failure and DNA damage
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: Mutant cells exhibited defective cytokinesis and increased DNA damage; altered actin binding observed

title: The puzzling duality of mesenchymal stem cells and adipocytes in bone marrow and ageing
task: Conceptualize strategies to restore bone marrow MSC niche and regulate MSC-adipocyte axis during aging
key_results: Proposed activation of pluripotent precursor cells, breakdown of BM-MSC grafts, and mobilization of extramedullary MSCs as mechanisms to restock BM-MSC niche and manage BMAT expansion
comments: This is a perspective/opinion piece lacking empirical data; the proposed strategies require experimental validation to assess feasibility and efficacy in aging models.

title: POU1F1 induces cancer stem cell-like traits in breast cancer cells by IL-6/JAK2/STAT3 activation and enrichment of ALDH
task: Elucidate POU1F1-mediated cancer stem cell induction in breast cancer
key_results: POU1F1 overexpression increased ALDH+ cells and enhanced mammosphere formation; IL-6/JAK2/STAT3 inhibitors decreased ALDH expression and colony formation
comments: The mechanistic link between POU1F1 and IL-6/JAK2/STAT3 activation is demonstrated but the direct transcriptional targets remain incompletely characterized.

model_io.model: POU1F1-overexpressing and knockdown MCF-7 breast cancer cells

hypotheses.hypothesis: POU1F1 induces BCSC phenotype via IL-6/JAK2/STAT3 pathway activation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: JAK inhibitors and anti-IL-6R antibodies reduced ALDH expression and mammosphere formation

title: Overcoming vascular niche–mediated TKI resistance in acute myeloid leukemia through miR-126 inhibition
task: Overcome TKI resistance in AML by targeting vascular niche-mediated miR-126 transfer to leukemic stem cells
key_results: Agent-based model demonstrated that TNF-α suppresses endothelial miR-126 production during blast expansion; post-TKI blast reduction restores EC miR-126 enabling LSC re-entry into quiescence; miR-126 inhibitor combined with TKI enhanced LSC eradication
comments: The computational model elegantly predicts niche dynamics, but clinical validation of the miRisten-TKI combination schedule in AML patients remains necessary.

model_io.model: Agent-based computational model of AML bone marrow microenvironment
In vitro AML cell lines and endothelial co-culture

hypotheses.hypothesis: Expanding blasts suppress EC miR-126; TKI-mediated blast reduction restores miR-126 promoting LSC quiescence and relapse
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: Model simulations showed dynamic miR-126 modulation driving niche-mediated TKI resistance

hypotheses.hypothesis: Combining TKI with miR-126 inhibitor disrupts protective niche enhancing LSC eradication
hypotheses.result: Confirmed in simulations: Combination dismantled protective niche and enhanced LSC eradication

title: Establishing a long-term versatile culture system for functional mouse mammary organoids from single cells
task: Develop long-term mammary organoid system recapitulating in vivo morphogenesis, functional cycles, and lineage plasticity for breast biology and cancer studies
key_results: Single basal stem cells generate 'mini-glands' replicating branched architecture and developmental stages (puberty, pregnancy, lactation, involution) in ~2 weeks; system enables lineage tracing and oncogenic transformation modeling
comments: The protocol successfully bridges in vitro and in vivo complexity with excellent morphological fidelity, though functional assessment of milk production capacity and hormonally-responsive gene expression kinetics require deeper validation.

model_io.model: Mouse primary mammary basal stem cells cultured in 3D organoid system

hypotheses.hypothesis: Single basal stem cells recapitulate in vivo mammary gland development and functional cycles in 3D culture
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 'Mini-glands' replicated branched architecture and developmental stages within ~2 weeks

title: A zebrafish multimodal toolbox to study the blood-brain barrier in health and disease
task: Establish zebrafish in vivo model to investigate blood-brain/retinal barrier dysfunction and tight junction disruption in hyperglycemia
key_results: Hyperglycemia increased midbrain vessel diameter and tracer leakage without affecting larval survival; hyperglycemia reduced claudin-5 and increased PLVAP indicating compromised tight junctions; TEM revealed ultrastructural junction defects
comments: The hyperglycemia-induced barrier disruption is well-characterized at molecular and structural levels, but caveolae quantification remains incomplete limiting conclusions about transcytosis mechanisms.

model_io.model: Hyperglycemia-exposed zebrafish larvae with fluorescent barrier reporters

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hyperglycemia reduces claudin-5 and increases PLVAP compromising BBB/BRB tight junction integrity
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: Hyperglycemia reduced claudin-5 and increased PLVAP; tracer leakage increased

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hyperglycemia induces ultrastructural tight junction defects
hypotheses.result: Partially confirmed: TEM revealed junction defects; caveolae quantification incomplete

title: Exploring the dual role of extracellular vesicles in coagulation and immune modulation in glioblastoma
task: Investigate dual roles of extracellular vesicles in glioblastoma-associated thrombosis and immune modulation
key_results: GBM tumor tissues showed high TF/PDPN expression with low TFPI (p<0.001); GBM-derived EVs exhibited procoagulant activity proportional to TF expression and modulated microglial senescence and immune polarization
comments: The work comprehensively links EV-mediated coagulation and immunity but lacks direct evidence that EV-targeting therapeutically prevents VTE complications in vivo.

model_io.model: Patient-derived GBM tissue and cell lines

hypotheses.hypothesis: GBM-derived EVs promote hypercoagulation via TF and PDPN transfer to circulating cells
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: EV procoagulant activity correlated with TF expression

hypotheses.hypothesis: GBM-derived EVs modulate immune responses independently of coagulation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: EVs induced microglial senescence and immune polarization in cell line-dependent manner

title: A robust mouse liver organoid platform enables sustained multicellular maturation and fibrosis modeling from a single tissue sample
task: Develop robust liver organoid platform for fibrosis modeling incorporating hepatocytes, cholangiocytes, and stellate cells
key_results: Multi-cellular organoids with Notch inhibitor and dexamethasone sustained albumin production and liver-specific gene expression over multiple passages; TGFβ-activated HSCs induced EMT and impaired hepatocyte/cholangiocyte proliferation recapitulating fibrosis
comments: The system successfully maintains mature hepatobiliary functions while modeling fibrosis, though metabolic capacity and drug metabolism kinetics require further validation against primary tissue.

model_io.model: 3D multi-cellular liver organoids from primary mouse hepatocytes, cholangiocytes, and HSCs

hypotheses.hypothesis: Notch inhibition and dexamethasone sustain organoid maturity and hepatobiliary function
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: Stable albumin production and liver-specific gene expression over multiple passages

hypotheses.hypothesis: TGFβ-activated HSCs induce hepatocyte/cholangiocyte EMT and growth inhibition modeling fibrosis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: Activated HSCs impaired proliferation and induced EMT in epithelial compartments

title: Raloxifene relaxes the rat aorta via a mitochondria-dependent mechanism
task: Identify mitochondria-dependent mechanism of raloxifene-induced vascular relaxation independent of estrogen receptors
key_results: Mitochondrial ROS inhibition (rotenone, mito-tempo) attenuated raloxifene-induced vasodilation; raloxifene suppressed phenylephrine-induced mitochondrial ROS and prevented UCP2 downregulation in vascular smooth muscle cells
comments: The mitochondrial ROS and UCP2 regulation is clearly demonstrated, but the upstream target or sensor initiating these effects remains unidentified.

model_io.model: Rat aortic rings with and without endothelium
Cultured rat vascular smooth muscle cells

hypotheses.hypothesis: Raloxifene-induced vasodilation is mediated by mitochondrial ROS modulation independent of estrogen receptors
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: Estrogen receptor antagonist did not block effect; mitochondrial ROS inhibitors attenuated vasodilation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Raloxifene prevents phenylephrine-induced mitochondrial ROS and UCP2 suppression
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: Raloxifene inhibited PE-induced ROS increase and prevented UCP2 downregulation

title: SPIONs-labeled hUCMSCs for in vitro safety analysis and in vivo tracking in scarred monkey uteri
task: Evaluate safety and efficacy of SPION-labeled hUCMSCs for MRI tracking in scarred uterine tissue repair
key_results: SPIONs effectively labeled hUCMSCs without compromising proliferation, differentiation, or surface marker expression; SPION labeling persisted >600 days enabling long-term MRI monitoring; no cytotoxicity observed
comments: The labeling and imaging are well-characterized, but functional assessment of uterine tissue repair and restoration of contractility following hUCMSC transplantation remains underdeveloped.

model_io.model: SPION-labeled hUCMSCs injected into scarred monkey uteri

hypotheses.hypothesis: SPION labeling preserves hUCMSC functionality and enables sustained MRI tracking
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: Labeled cells retained proliferation, differentiation, and viability with labeling persisting >600 days

title: Functional protein design and enhancement with ontology reinforcement iteration
task: Engineer proteins with enhanced functionality through ontology-guided design and feedback loops
key_results: Lysozyme variant with 100-fold activity increase; chitinase stable at 85°C; dual-function enzyme with lysozyme and chitinase activities
comments: Closed-loop iterative design bridges computational modeling and wet-lab validation but scalability to larger variant spaces unclear.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Ontology-conditioned decoding with RLWF enables multi-objective protein optimization
hypotheses.result: Achieved substantial improvements across enzymatic activity, thermal stability, and multifunctionality

title: Tumor-specific lncRNA IGF1R-AS1 trans-regulates chromatin interactions associated with oncogenic MYC signaling
task: Characterize trans-acting lncRNA regulation of oncogenic MYC through chromatin looping
key_results: IGF1R-AS1 identified in 1,344 lncRNAs; facilitates long-range MYC enhancer-promoter looping; overexpressed in prostate and lung cancers
comments: Non-canonical lncRNA mechanism provides mechanistic insight but functional validation limited to computational/molecular approaches.

hypotheses.hypothesis: IGF1R-AS1 trans-acts with chromatin remodeling complexes to enhance MYC expression
hypotheses.result: Demonstrated non-canonical trans-acting role facilitating distal MYC enhancer-promoter looping

title: Genomic inference of sites of transmission during regional spread of blaNDM Klebsiella pneumoniae in Michigan
task: Infer facility transmission sources for regional healthcare-associated pathogen spread using genomic epidemiology
key_results: Single focal facility implicated in sustained transmission of carbapenem-resistant K. pneumoniae across 72 patients in Michigan; identified transmission sites prior to case detection
comments: Genomics-informed surveillance enables real-time intervention targeting; approach sensitive to healthcare exposure completeness and phylogenetic uncertainty.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Shared healthcare exposures among phylogenetically linked cases identify facility transmission sources
hypotheses.result: Successfully implicated focal facility and predicted upstream transmission sites prior to case detection

title: Cell Type Populations for 3D Anatomical Structures of the Human Reference Atlas
task: Quantify cell type populations across 3D anatomical structures
key_results: 27.6 million cells mapped across 73 organs and 1,283 anatomical structures; integrated 662 spatial-transcriptomics and proteomics datasets
comments: Provides comprehensive reference framework but relies on heterogeneous datasets with potential batch effects and variable annotation quality.

outputs: cell type populations per anatomical structure

hypotheses.hypothesis: Integrated sc-transcriptomics and sc-proteomics yield consistent cell type assignments
hypotheses.result: 558 transcriptomics and 104 proteomics datasets successfully integrated with generalizability validation

title: MIrROR release 02: Expanded and refined 16S-ITS-23S rRNA operon dataset
task: Expand and refine 16S-ITS-23S rRNA operon dataset for microbial identification
key_results: MIrROR v2 covers 476,579 sequences, 249,907 genomes, 29,051 species (387.39%, 472.49%, 206.28% increases); added archaeal genomes; improved mock community performance
comments: Comprehensive curation pipeline addresses redundancy and contamination; GTDB taxonomy reclassification increases consistency but impacts backward compatibility.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Expanded dataset with improved curation enhances microbial identification accuracy
hypotheses.result: Demonstrated improved performance in mock community analyses; 206-472% increase in covered genomes/species

title: Design of a global population-covering multi-epitope mRNA vaccine against Lassa virus using immunoinformatics
task: Design multi-epitope mRNA vaccine against Lassa virus with global population coverage
key_results: 99.98% global population coverage; secondary structure quality 82.4% Ramachandran; strong TLR-2/TLR-4 binding; predicted IL-2 and IFN-γ activation
comments: Comprehensive immunoinformatics pipeline but lacks experimental validation; animal studies required for safety/potency assessment.

inputs: Lassa virus protein sequences (GP, NP, ZP)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-epitope design with adjuvants achieves global population coverage and immunogenicity
hypotheses.result: 99.98% MHC coverage with favorable structural quality and immune simulation responses

title: Evaluation of phytochemicals from Tamarindus indica as a potential catechol-O-methyltransferase (COMT) inhibitor: an in-silico approach for Parkinson’s disease
task: Evaluate Tamarindus indica phytochemicals as COMT inhibitors for Parkinson's disease
key_results: Gallacetophenone identified from 170 phytochemicals as promising COMT inhibitor; enhanced conformational stability in MD simulations; favorable drug-likeness properties
comments: Natural product screening reduces hepatotoxicity risk but computational prediction requires in vitro/in vivo validation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Plant-derived COMT inhibitors provide safer alternatives to synthetic inhibitors
hypotheses.result: Gallacetophenone demonstrated stable COMT binding, enhanced conformational stability, and favorable pharmacokinetic properties in silico

title: Integrated proteomic and single-cell transcriptomic profiling elucidates immunomodulatory effects of L-serine in autism spectrum disorder
task: Elucidate immunomodulatory effects of L-serine in autism spectrum disorder via proteomics and single-cell transcriptomics
key_results: 12-week L-serine treatment improved behavioral measures; shifted naive CD4 T cell transcriptional signatures toward typical state; altered immune-related plasma protein pathways
comments: Integrative proteome-transcriptome approach provides mechanistic insight; small cohort size and lack of placebo control limit generalizability.

hypotheses.hypothesis: L-serine reshapes adaptive immune cell states in ASD patients
hypotheses.result: Demonstrated favorable shifts in CD4 T cell transcriptional profiles and immune-related protein pathways alongside behavioral improvements

title: Virtual screening and molecular dynamics simulations for drug repurposing against autophagy to attenuate blast in cereal plants
task: Identify fungal autophagy inhibitors for blast disease via virtual screening and MD simulations
key_results: Six FDA-approved drugs identified as Atg4 binders (31 h-Phthalocyanine, rebastinib, flumatinib, zafirlukast, beigene-283, radotinib); 18 µs MD simulations validated binding stability
comments: Drug repurposing approach accelerates development timeline; Atg4 inhibition mechanism validated computationally but requires fungal viability assays.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Virtual screening and MD simulations identify stable FDA-approved drug-Atg4 binders
hypotheses.result: Six candidate compounds with distinct binding modes; sustained hydrogen bonding and favorable binding energies across simulation trajectories

title: Optimization and comparison research on adaptive modularization BERT-SPAN model for named entity recognition of SARS-CoV-2 virus proteins
task: Optimize named entity recognition for SARS-CoV-2 viral proteins using adaptive modular neural networks
key_results: BERT-AM-FFN-SPAN achieves 93.66% F1 score; surpasses BERT-SPAN (91.85%) and BERT-BiLSTM-CRF (80.81%)
comments: Addresses domain-specific NER challenge but limited to single protein entity type; corpus size and annotation methodology not detailed.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Adaptive modular FFN with attention mechanism improves viral protein entity recognition
hypotheses.result: AM-FFN-SPAN variant outperformed standard BERT-SPAN and BERT-BiLSTM-CRF models

title: Entropy-regularized dual-stream attention fusion for multi-disease lung classification
task: Classify multiple lung diseases using dual-stream attention fusion of CNNs and vision transformers
key_results: ADSAF achieves 98.3% accuracy; outperforms CNN, transformer, and ensemble baselines; robust to noisy conditions and domain shifts; Grad-CAM localizes clinically meaningful regions
comments: Strong multi-disease classification performance; adaptive fusion mechanism addresses static fusion limitations effectively.

inputs: pneumonia and COVID-19 chest imaging datasets

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dynamic attention fusion of CNN and transformer streams improves multi-disease lung classification
hypotheses.result: Achieved 98.3% accuracy with superior robustness and generalization; clinically meaningful attention maps

title: Integrated machine learning and multi-omics analysis identifies ALOX5 as a potential therapeutic target for tubulointerstitial inflammation in diabetic kidney disease
task: Identify key inflammatory biomarkers in diabetic kidney disease tubulointerstitial inflammation
key_results: ALOX5 identified as crucial feature gene; upregulated in DKD tissues; high expression correlates with decreased GFR and M1 macrophage infiltration; honokiol identified as potential ALOX5 inhibitor
comments: Multi-omics integration robust but candidate compound validation limited to computational prediction; requires experimental confirmation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: ALOX5 mediates pro-inflammatory macrophage polarization through leukotriene-NF-κB signaling in DKD
hypotheses.result: High ALOX5/ALOX5AP co-localization with NF-κB/p65 and iNOS in CD68+ macrophages; ALOX5 associated with decreased GFR

title: Identification and clustering analysis of drug-responsive temporally varying genes through high-frequency longitudinal RNA sequencing
task: Identify and cluster temporally varying genes in drug response using high-frequency longitudinal RNA-seq
key_results: Three distinct temporal patterns identified; 186 conserved hepatotoxicity signature genes across all compounds; dose-dependent kinetic-pharmacodynamic modeling enabled
comments: Novel approach captures dynamic transcriptional responses missed by standard DE analysis; limited to hepatotoxicity in rodent model.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Variance-based scoring detects temporal gene dynamics missed by static differential expression
hypotheses.result: Identified three temporal response patterns with distinct biological process enrichments and dose-dependent trajectories

title: Estimating major pathological response in non-small cell lung cancer patients with post-neoadjuvant therapy using MMT-net
task: Estimate major pathological response in lung cancer post-neoadjuvant therapy from whole-slide images

title: Calcaneal spur detection from lateral foot radiographs using deep feature engineering

key_results: 93.42% accuracy achieved on 775 X-ray images (316 normal, 459 with spur) using CNN-INCA-tkNN-IMV-greedy ensemble
comments: Addresses small biomedical dataset challenge effectively; framework combines multiple dimensionality reduction and ensemble strategies but computational complexity high.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-stage feature engineering (CNN extraction + INCA selection + tkNN ensemble + IMV voting) achieves high accuracy on small datasets
hypotheses.result: 93.42% accuracy with higher sensitivity for spur detection than normal heel detection

title: Population genetic variation characterised through serial independent pool-seq: the Cyprus Genome Project

key_results: >4 million variants identified; >100,000 novel variants absent from gnomAD v4.1/ClinVar; >0.92 concordance with published cohorts; significant local-global allele frequency differences
comments: Pool-seq design enables cost-effective large cohort sequencing with empirical confidence intervals; notable geographic variation in pathogenic variant frequencies.

inputs: DNA from 10,000 bone marrow donors (10 independent pools)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Serial independent pool-seq with 10 replicates distinguishes true variants from sequencing artifacts
hypotheses.result: Identified >100,000 novel variants; validated against clinical cohorts (r>0.92); revealed population-specific frequency patterns

title: Changes in the proteomic profile of athletes’ plasma associated with exercise intensity

title: Randomised study of human machine collaboration for cardiotocography interpretation during labour

comments: Rigorous randomized multi-reader design; cCTG correct 67.5% in discordant cases, suggesting room for improvement.

hypotheses.hypothesis: cCTG assistance improves clinician detection of perinatal acidemia and inter-observer agreement

title: Framework to completely bypass expensive DFT calculations via graph neural networks for vacancy formation energy predictions in FCC high entropy alloys

title: Accelerating atomic fine structure determination with graph reinforcement learning

key_results: Hundreds of energy levels identified in hours; 95% agreement with published Co II values; 54–87% for Nd species

hypotheses.hypothesis: MDP formulation + learned reward functions accelerate atomic data determination

title: Coexisting kagome and heavy fermion flat bands in YbCr6Ge6
task: Identify coexisting kagome and heavy fermion flat bands in YbCr6Ge6
key_results: Intrinsic kagome flat band at high temperature; upon cooling, Yb 4f hybridization creates momentum-independent Kondo resonances; topological weak/strong Kondo-insulating regimes and topological Dirac–Kondo semimetal identified
comments: Important discovery unifying topology with heavy-fermion physics; experimental techniques (ARPES, other spectroscopies) not detailed—confirmation by independent probes would strengthen conclusions.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Yb 4f states hybridize with topological kagome flat bands upon cooling
hypotheses.result: High-temperature kagome flat band transforms into momentum-independent Kondo resonances; topological properties preserved

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hybridization gaps support tunable topological phases
hypotheses.result: Filling-dependent weak and strong topological Kondo-insulating regimes and topological Dirac–Kondo semimetal states identified

title: Epsilon-near-zero time-gate for high-fidelity spatial information transfer through dynamic scattering media
task: High-fidelity spatial information transfer through scattering media using time-gating

model_io.model: Epsilon-near-zero four-wave mixing in ITO film

hypotheses.hypothesis: Time-gating via sum-FWM in sub-wavelength ENZ film removes scattered photons

task: Infrared imaging and Fourier filtering using visible-wavelength spatial light modulator
